page: Amazon Web Services m8idn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel processors with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The physical hardware configuration includes 48 physical cores with 2 threads per core, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance is equipped with 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing high-speed local storage directly attached to the host. For network-intensive operations, the m8idn.24xlarge delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8idn.24xlarge offers high resource density by combining substantial compute capacity, high-speed local NVMe storage, and high-throughput networking in a single general-purpose instance. This makes it suitable for demanding enterprise workloads that require balanced CPU and memory resources alongside rapid local storage access. Appropriate workloads include large-scale databases, data processing pipelines, high-performance web serving, and network-intensive applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8idn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs with an x86_64 Intel architecture running at 3.9 GHz, supported by 48 physical cores and 384.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage-heavy and network-intensive applications, the instance includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. It does not contain integrated GPU hardware. The m8idn.24xlarge is designed for resource-dense workloads requiring a balance of high-speed local storage, substantial memory, and high network throughput. It is well-suited for enterprise databases, data processing, and network-intensive applications.
